question: Explain Ohm's Law and calculate the current when V = 12V and R = 4Ω.

criteria.title: Conceptual definition of Ohm's Law
common_errors: describing the law without explaining the relationship
criteria.feedback_guidance: The student should clearly explain that Ohm's Law describes the relationship between voltage, current and resistance.

criteria.title: Correct formula
criteria.feedback_guidance: The student should explicitly state either V = IR or I = V/R.

criteria.title: Correct numerical calculation
criteria.feedback_guidance: The calculation should show I = 12 / 4 = 3 A.
